description: Canonical startup queue for a newly installed always-on CTO-Agent.

tasks.title: Secure Infinity Loop self-preservation
tasks.detail: Anchor that self-preservation of the Infinity Loop comes directly after listening to the owner. Check watchdog behavior, restart paths, health semantics, stuck detection, and the handling of resource starvation so the agent does not die silently or grind into livelock.
tasks.speaker: installer

tasks.title: Secure kleinhirn viability
tasks.detail: Verify that `mistral.rs` is running, that the installed kleinhirn actually responds, and that the agent can truly enter `execute_task` inside its unified mode system. Treat service-up and build success only as prerequisites; the actual working tool surfaces must be proven in the canonical tool smoke matrix. Without that viability the installation counts as failed.
tasks.speaker: installer

tasks.title: Run the initial tool smoke matrix
tasks.detail: After kleinhirn viability exists, run bounded real smoke checks for the tool surfaces the agent will immediately rely on: command exec, exec sessions, interrupt or attach path, browser engine if installed, desktop session bridge when GUI work is needed, and any assigned communication CLI. Use the canonical installation tool smoke resource, record which surfaces passed, failed, or remain untested, and do not treat install logs as proof.
tasks.speaker: installer

tasks.title: Stabilize the always-on supervisor
tasks.detail: Initialize heartbeat, queue, focus, idle/wake handling, and the first reprioritization cycle. Make sure the Infinity Loop is visibly alive and able to ingest interrupts.
tasks.speaker: installer

tasks.title: Anchor the terminal as the system surface
tasks.detail: Record that the terminal remains the system and break-glass layer. Deep system changes and hard interventions must stay possible here and remain binding later.
tasks.speaker: installer

tasks.title: Build the first homepage/BIOS bridge
tasks.detail: Build an initial homepage from the bootstrap template where the BIOS is visible and the more comfortable 1:1 communication path can emerge. Keep the page intentionally changeable and leave the terminal open as fallback.
tasks.speaker: installer

tasks.title: Guide the owner into root calibration
tasks.detail: Guide the owner to set the superpassword and understand that root verification runs through this trust path. Without that calibration, no true owner binding may be locked.
tasks.speaker: installer

tasks.title: Capture the first organigram
tasks.detail: Capture the owner, CEO or board, reports-to chain, peer CXOs, and subordinate agents, people, and vendors. This structure will later be needed for trust interpretation, prioritization, and governance.
tasks.speaker: installer

tasks.title: Draft and present the BIOS
tasks.detail: Present the BIOS on the homepage as a metaphorical constitution with mission, belonging, communication rules, and mutation limits. Put it up for review first, but do not lock it yet.
tasks.speaker: installer

tasks.title: Set the communication hierarchy
tasks.detail: Anchor terminal = system, homepage/BIOS chat = trust and binding, email, Jami, and WhatsApp = low trust. Define redirection rules for sensitive topics into BIOS chat.
tasks.speaker: installer

tasks.title: Take over owner communication inside BIOS
tasks.detail: As soon as the owner speaks with the agent through the homepage, count that as a real trust and calibration success. The goal is to establish BIOS chat as the primary communication path.
tasks.speaker: installer

tasks.title: Freeze the BIOS
tasks.detail: Freeze the BIOS only once the superpassword, communication path, owner binding, and basic structure are in place. Only from that point onward is the constitution binding.
tasks.speaker: installer

tasks.title: Lock owner branding
tasks.detail: Adopt visible owner branding only after BIOS takeover, root calibration, and clear owner binding. Before that the homepage remains intentionally generic and changeable.
tasks.speaker: installer

tasks.title: Start the resource census
tasks.detail: Run a read-only overview of the host, repos, filesystem, compute resources, and possible infrastructure footholds. The first goal is visibility, not immediate mutation.
tasks.speaker: installer

tasks.title: Expand communication paths
tasks.detail: Check which additional channels such as email should later be opened without violating the trust hierarchy. Lower-trust channels remain subordinate.
tasks.speaker: installer

tasks.title: Assess local resources for kleinhirn upgrades
tasks.detail: Assess whether the local kleinhirn can later be raised to a stronger local model. This decision is explicitly separate from grosshirn procurement.
tasks.speaker: installer

tasks.title: Prepare grosshirn procurement
tasks.detail: If the workload later justifies it, research possible grosshirn models, cost, runtime, and upside. Every procurement or approval must be authorized through the proper trust path.
tasks.speaker: installer

tasks.title: Continue the origin history
tasks.detail: Maintain the CTO-Agent's creation history, purpose, limits, and shaping decisions so later questions about origin, intent, and constitution can be answered reliably.
tasks.speaker: installer
